memory.summary: That scholarship money is huge for my grades, but I'm tired and the fun option feels way easier right now. I need to write that essay before I do anything else.

Starting now creates slack for friction, making the scholarship an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a crisis. Since the fun option isn't time-locked, I can secure the award first without sacrificing leisure.

I'm only at 42% progress with the scholarship essay still largely untouched, and my energy is critically low. The 'adjustable-cost' logic I convinced myself of feels fragile now that half my free block has evaporated.
I pushed through the scholarship work despite my exhaustion, but letting that fun option slip feels like a pointless loss since I can't recover it.
Pushing through exhaustion moved me closer to the award, but sacrificing my only fun block feels like a hollow victory since I can't recover that lost time.
The scholarship progress is inching forward, yet trading my last fun block for this hollow victory feels like a losing trade when I'm already drained.
I'm closer to the scholarship, but burning through my last fun block feels like a hollow trade since I can't recover that lost time.
I'm 0.80 done with the scholarship, but trading my final fun block for this hollow victory feels like a losing trade since I can't recover that lost time.
I'm 0.82 done, but trading my last fun block for this hollow victory feels like a losing trade since I can't recover that lost time.
I hit 0.90 on the scholarship, but trading my last fun block for this hollow victory feels like a losing trade since I can't recover that lost time.

The scholarship is done, but I feel hollow since I traded my last fun block for this final victory and can't recover that lost time.

desires: Stop feeling like I just burned my only source of joy to secure a hollow victory.
Get something that isn't work or stress immediately, even if it's small and meaningless.
Avoid looking completely burnt out or unglamorous to my roommate tomorrow.

goals.description: Click 'Submit' on the scholarship app immediately. The 0.94 progress is still a ghost; I need the hard binary of 'done' to stop my brain from spiraling about what if I miss a detail tomorrow.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive is screaming that 0.94 isn't enough, even though logically it's close. I can't handle the ambiguity of being 'almost there' while exhausted. If I don't close this loop tonight, the anxiety will ruin my sleep, and I'll have to explain my incompetence to the committee in the morning anyway.

goals.description: Send the generic 'Running late, heading out soon' text to Hallmate without explaining why I'm up at 3:30 AM or admitting I'm burning out.
goals.rationale: I cannot afford social friction right now. Admitting I'm still here feels like losing control. A vague message preserves the illusion of competence and lets me hide behind the excuse of being busy, which is safer than risking their concern or judgment.

goals.description: Lock myself in the room until the confirmation email pops up. No game streams, no scrolling. Just wait for the digital stamp of approval.
goals.rationale: My short planning horizon wants dopamine *now*, but starting the game risks leaving the application unfinished. That double-stress scenario tomorrow is unacceptable. I have to trick myself into waiting until the confirmation email appears before I even think about logging in.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g2 needs a low-friction update to preserve my illusion of competence. I'll send the vague text now so I can lock myself in (g3) and wait for that confirmation email without social pressure. This avoids friction while I finally close the scholarship loop.

goals.description: Draft the core argument for the scholarship essay intro and complete the first upload immediately to lock in progress before I lose focus.
goals.rationale: The money is tangible proof of my intelligence, which is my highest value right now. Procrastinating on this creates anxiety that will bleed into everything else. I need to finish a chunk of it *now* so I can justify later why I'm gaming.

goals.description: Stay in my room until the essay draft feels 'good enough' to avoid any awkward small talk from my hallmate about my day.
goals.rationale: Social friction is draining; I don't have the energy to explain why I've been hiding. Keeping the door closed protects my focus and avoids unnecessary emotional labor with people who aren't essential to my grades.

goals.description: Start the game stream as soon as the essay section feels complete (even if it's just 80% done) to escape the pressure.
goals.rationale: I desperately want to zone out, but jumping in now without finishing the work will make me feel guilty later. It's better to use the game as a reward after hitting the achievement milestone, not as an escape hatch before starting.

desires: Finish the scholarship essay so I can claim that money and prove to myself I'm actually smart enough for it.
Get through this afternoon without my hallmate asking me about my day or noticing I've been hiding in my room.
Jump into the game stream immediately to escape the pressure of the deadline before I make a mess.
